message:
feat(fleet): implement FileFleetTransport with file-based action log and node state

Add the concrete file-based implementation of the FleetTransport trait,
enabling distributed fleet coordination via filesystem-backed state:

FileFleetTransport core:
- File-based fleet action log (JSONL append with advisory file locking)
- Node status persistence with atomic temp-file swap (write to .tmp,
  rename into place) to prevent partial-read corruption
- Advisory file locking with 3-step exponential backoff (100/200/400ms)
  to handle concurrent agent access without hard failures
- Node lock files per node_id for fine-grained write serialization

Key operations:
- initialize(): creates root/nodes/locks directories and seeds empty
  action log — idempotent, safe to call multiple times
- append_action(): lock → append JSONL record → unlock
- read_action_log(): lock-shared → parse all records → unlock
- write/read_node_status(): per-node lock → atomic write/read → unlock
- list_node_statuses(): scan nodes/ directory for all .json status files
- list_stale_nodes(): filter node statuses by staleness threshold
- validate_node_id(): rejects empty, oversized (>128 char), and
  path-traversal-containing node IDs

Error handling:
- FleetTransportError with typed variants (Io, Serialization,
  NotInitialized, StaleState, InvalidInput) and Display/Error impls
- Lock errors surface both the error kind and the file path for debugging

Also adds LOCK_RETRY_BACKOFF_MILLIS constant and helper functions:
lock_file_with_backoff(), unlock_file(), parse_jsonl_records()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
